title: Corybas macranthus
taxonomy: Corybas macranthus is a species of orchid. This plant is classified within the genus Corybas, which belongs to the subfamily Diurideae. Corybas macranthus is further classified under the family Orchidaceae and the order Asparagales. The classification of this orchid is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Monocots, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Subfamily: Diurideae, Tribe: Diurideae, Genus: Corybas, Species: C. macranthus. Corybas macranthus is native to Australia and New Guinea.

intro: Corybas macranthus, also known as the 'large-flowered helmet orchid', is a species of orchid endemic to Western Australia, characterized by its unique helmet-shaped flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Corybas macranthus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Corybas macranthus is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and tinctures.
